content: Do you consider a short in Vestas a good option?

args.thought: Frame the short thesis question: Is VWS.CO a compelling short? Need to assess: (1) valuation gap magnitude, (2) catalyst timing and certainty, (3) risk/reward asymmetry, (4) practical shorting considerations for European stock, (5) what could invalidate the bear case.

Continue short thesis framework: From memory, VWS.CO fair value 145-165 DKK vs current ~172 DKK = modest 4-16% downside. Need to verify current price, check if valuation gap has widened/narrowed, and identify specific near-term catalysts. Key risks to short: (1) order backlog growth, (2) margin recovery from service business, (3) policy support tailwinds, (4) M&A speculation. Need fresh data on: current price, Q4 2025 / FY2025 results, 2026 guidance, order book trends, service margin trajectory.

## Narrative Update The analyst price target for Vestas Wind Systems has been raised by DKK 13.40 to DKK 175.45, as analysts factor in updated assumptions around fair value, revenue growth, profit margins and future P/E, along with recent Street research pointing to higher targets from DKK 170 to DKK 185 and mixed views on the stock’s rating.   ...    Recent Street research on Vestas Wind Systems reflects a mix of optimism on valuation potential and caution on execution risk, with price targets now clustered around the DKK 170 to DKK 185 range and ratings split between more   ...    Bullish Takeaways - Overall, bullish research underpins the view that recent Street work is incrementally supportive of the stock’s   ...    - These higher targets generally assume that Vestas can support the revenue and margin assumptions embedded in the revised fair value model, which would help justify a stronger P/E profile over time. - The move to DKK 185 at JPMorgan and other bullish targets supports the idea that current pricing already reflects   ...    expectations. - Bullish analysts see room for upside toward the higher end of the DKK 170 to DKK 185 target range, which feeds into   ...    - This split between higher targets and more restrained ratings reinforces that, while the consensus target has moved   ...    - The company issued earnings guidance for fiscal 2026, with revenue expected in a range of €20b to €22b and an EBIT   ...    ## Valuation Changes - Future P/E: 18.48x vs 19.20x, slightly lower, indicating a marginally less demanding earnings multiple in the updated assumptions. - Net Profit Margin: 6.26% vs 5.79%, moderately higher, pointing to a more optimistic view on future profitability. - Revenue Growth: 8.08% vs 7.99%, essentially unchanged, with only a small upward adjustment to projected growth. - Discount Rate: 7.89% vs 7.43%, modestly higher, which usually implies a slightly higher required return from investors. - Fair Value: DKK 175.45 vs DKK 162.05, slightly higher, reflecting updated assumptions in the model.   ...  When allocating Vestas’s capital, we apply the following principles while ensuring that Vestas remains resilient to economic fluctua-tions, respecting the volatile cycles of our industry :  
 • Reinvest into our existing business, including R&D, to deliver on our strategy and vision. 
 • Make value-creating acquisitions to accelerate or increase profitable growth. 
 • We are committed to maintaining a solid investment grade profile, targeting NIBD/EBITDA between -1x and 1x through the cycle.

args.thought: Synthesize short thesis assessment: The data shows my previous fair value estimate (145-165 DKK) is now BELOW current market price (~172-176 DKK) AND below analyst consensus (175-220 DKK). FY2025 showed genuine improvement: revenue +9% to EUR 18.8B, EBIT margin 5.7% (upper end of guidance), net income +58% to EUR 780M. 2026 guidance (EUR 20-22B revenue, 6-8% EBIT margin) implies continued margin expansion. Record backlog of EUR 71.9B provides visibility. The short thesis has weakened - valuation gap closed, momentum positive, analysts raising targets (Goldman 220, HSBC 200, Berenberg 175). Key short risks: (1) margin recovery trajectory intact, (2) service business stabilizing at 15.5-17.5% EBIT guidance, (3) policy tailwinds (US IRA confirmed, EU renewable push). Bears would need to argue: 2026 guidance is unachievable, offshore ramp fails, tariff headwinds worsen, or service margins don't recover. These are possible but not high-probability near-term catalysts. Conclusion: Short is NOT compelling at current levels - risk/reward asymmetric against the short.
